4: passed over. (See Ex. 12:22-23.) Later, Moses used hyssop in sprinkling the blood of the testament on the scriptures and on the people. (See Ex. 24:8; Heb. 9:19-20.) David, aching to be cleansed, pleaded, "Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean." (Ps. 51:7.) (Kelly Ogden, "A Sampler of Biblical Plants," Ensign, Aug. 1990, 37-38)

The cedar represented pride and haughtiness, whereas the hyssop symbolized modesty, humility, and purity. Leviticus 14 details its use in the cleansing process for a leper.
